mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate acts as a discount-rate shock that tightens financial conditions and compresses equity multiples, so higher FEDFUNDS changes predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if rising FEDFUNDS episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays above 0.00 instead of negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE indicates deteriorating household income and weaker future demand, so unemployment increases over a quarter predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rise triggers are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the signal's out-of-sample Rank IC is not negative across any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals broad-based labor income and retail demand strength, which should support corporate revenues and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS improvement does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered instances yield negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls below a low-growth threshold, it signals housing demand contraction and a broader consumption slowdown that tends to pressure equities over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown triggers are not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO combined with a rise in CPIAUCSL indicates stagflationary pressure, where weaker real output and sticky inflation undermine margins and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined weak-production/high-inflation state does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term is not materially more negative than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS still low implies real policy rates are falling behind inflation, which can lift nominal revenue expectations and support positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its 6-month average while FEDFUNDS remains below 3%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration episodes with low FEDFUNDS are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction effect is not positive out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening-cycle environment, a stable or falling PAYEMS reading indicates labor market resilience despite policy restraint, which should cushion earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has risen in each of the last 2 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if payroll stability during recent tightening does not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the triggered win rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDP growth print relative to the recent trend supports earnings revisions and risk appetite, so accelerating GDPC1 should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is above its previous 2-quarter average
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend GDPC1 growth is not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or if the rolling 5-year Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero.
